brief_summary: Two contrasting supply-chain diagrams. Left (2018-2022, warm-grey): designer → host vendor → five regional proxies fanning out to buyer; bordeaux footer notes the 8-month March-to-November latency. Right (2026, plum): a single vendor configurator block (Mode, CannonKeys, Qwertykeys, Wuque) shipping direct to buyer; plum footer notes in-stock cadence and no region footnote.
data_sources: vendor-first-customs.mdx § 'The proxy era, briefly' — 'an interest check on GeekHack, a host vendor for the global region, and a set of regional proxies — Oblotzky and MyKeyboard.eu for Europe, Cerakeys for Asia-Pacific, a rotating cast for South America ... A buyer who decided in March that they wanted a custom typically had a board in hand in November.'
vendor-first-customs.mdx § 'Mode set the configurator template' — 'The configurator is the supply chain ... no proxy. There's no check your region footnote.'
vendor-first-customs.mdx § 'CannonKeys and Qwertykeys shipped in-house' — naming CannonKeys, Qwertykeys, Wuque Studio as parallel examples
